hiddenCosts.name: Site Selection Area Change Request Fee

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $522,952 mid-point build-out, there is a $272,952 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptLabel: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)

benchmark.label: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Blended margin. The bar/kitchen attach rate swings COGS up; the large footprint plus simulator/equipment R&M swing fixed costs. Owner-operator vs. absentee is a full GM salary either way.
benchmark.rampNote: 6–18 months to a mature membership/booking base; the Item 19 average is NOT a year-one number.
considerations: Budget blended COGS ~15–28% (sim time and memberships carry little; the bar and kitchen drive it) and labor ~22–30%.
Decide owner-operator vs. absentee before modeling — absentee adds a GM salary (~$60–90K/yr) the pro forma omits.
Confirm the F&B attach rate and per-bay equipment costs (e.g. simulator licenses, R&M) at mature franchised units via Item 20.
Assume a 6–18 month ramp; discount the Item 19 mature average for year one.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 15–26% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an automated / minimal-staff model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 5–12% — the 22–30% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as automated / minimal-staff from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: This franchisor is losing money as it grows (a net loss of $230K in the most recent year), though it still has positive net worth. Worth understanding how long its runway lasts.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing; positive net worth. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by DA Advisory Group PLLC): Net loss of $230K on $658K of revenue — roughly 1.3x spent per $1 earned. Losses are widening, not shrinking. Positive net worth of $153K. Cash of $465K against $309K of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 3.73). Note: about 63% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.3x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Zeze Holdings, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
